name: Dr. Phil Roe
tweet: Looking back on his life, we should all be thankful for Sam Johnson’s relentless heart of service. This is a huge loss for the great state of Texas, and it was an honor to serve with him.

name: Dr. Phil Roe
tweet: I remember how he had difficulty punching his voting card on the House floor because he had been injured so badly in the war, but he always showed up for his country.

name: Dr. Phil Roe
tweet: Sam told me that when the military was testing the effects of nuclear explosions on planes, he volunteered to fly over 5 nuclear explosions. Sam went on to become a respected conservative leader in Congress.

name: Dr. Phil Roe
tweet: After being shot down in Vietnam in 1966, Sam was taken prisoner and tortured for nearly 7 YEARS – including 2.5 years spent in leg irons. After all that, he immediately showed up in line, reporting for duty.

name: Dr. Phil Roe
tweet: My heart breaks for the loss of my former colleague and a true American hero, Sam Johnson. Sam flew 62 missions as a Thunderbird, the Air Force’s world-renowned precision flying demonstration team, during the Korean War and 25 missions during the Vietnam War.
